title: jacquemontia subsessilis
intro: Jacquemontia subsessilis, also known as the climbing morning glory, is a perennial vine plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Jacquemontia subsessilis include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Jacquemontia subsessilis typically involve the leaves and stems being boiled or infused in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
